definitions.example: Muscle tissue is responsible for movement and contraction in the body.
definitions.definition: A group of similar cells that perform a specific function. Tissues are the building blocks of organs.

definitions.example: The heart is an organ that pumps blood throughout the body.
definitions.definition: A self-contained part of an organism that performs a specific function. Organs are made up of two or more types of tissues.

definitions.example: The nervous system is an organ system that includes the brain, spinal cord, and nerves.
definitions.definition: A group of organs that work together to perform a specific function or set of functions.

definitions.example: Skin cells are the outermost layer of the skin, protecting the body from external damage.
definitions.definition: The basic structural and functional unit of life. Cells are the building blocks of tissues and organs.

definitions.example: The cytoplasm of a cell contains the mitochondria, where cellular respiration occurs.
definitions.definition: The jelly-like substance inside a cell where many metabolic reactions take place.

definitions.example: Mitochondria are found in the cytoplasm of cells and are essential for their survival.
definitions.definition: The powerhouse of the cell, responsible for generating energy through cellular respiration.

definitions.example: The nucleus of a cell contains the genetic material that determines its characteristics.
definitions.definition: The control center of the cell where DNA is stored, responsible for cell growth and reproduction.

definitions.example: Protoplasm is essential for cell function and survival.
definitions.definition: The living matter of a cell, consisting of cytoplasm and nucleus.

definitions.example: Muscle tissue is organized into different types of fibers to facilitate movement.
definitions.definition: The arrangement of cells in a tissue to perform a specific function.

definitions.example: The epithelial tissue in the lungs helps to exchange oxygen and carbon dioxide.
definitions.definition: A type of tissue that forms the lining of organs and glands, regulating the exchange of substances.

definitions.example: Connective tissue in the skin provides cushioning and protection.
definitions.definition: A type of tissue that provides support and structure to the body, including bones, cartilage, and fat.

definitions.example: Muscle tissue in the arm enables movement and flexion.
definitions.definition: A type of tissue that enables movement and contraction in the body.

definitions.example: Nervous tissue in the brain enables thought and cognitive function.
definitions.definition: A type of tissue that transmits and processes information in the body.

definitions.example: Glandular tissue in the pancreas produces digestive enzymes.
definitions.definition: A type of tissue that produces and secretes hormones or other substances.

definitions.example: Histology is used to diagnose diseases and understand tissue function.
definitions.definition: The study of the microscopic structure of tissues and cells.

definitions.example: Anatomy is essential for understanding how the body functions and responds to disease.
definitions.definition: The study of the structure and organization of living organisms.

definitions.example: Microscopic organisation is used to understand the function and behavior of cells and tissues.
definitions.definition: The study of the structure and organization of cells and tissues at a microscopic level.

definitions.example: Body organisation is essential for understanding how the body functions and responds to disease.
definitions.definition: The study of the structure and organization of the body as a whole.

definitions.example: Integration occurs at various levels, from cells to organs and systems.
definitions.definition: The process by which different parts of the body work together to maintain homeostasis and overall health.

definitions.example: Homeostasis is essential for maintaining overall health and well-being.
definitions.definition: The ability of the body to maintain a stable internal environment despite changes in external conditions.
